# Tiles Privacy

> Runs on-device models with encrypted P2P sync, keeping your data and identity local, and supports sharing chats publicly on ATProto.

Last updated: 2026-04-25
This file follows the llms.txt convention and provides a complete content map for tiles.run.
Use the links below to fetch canonical page content; each list item includes a short note for quick routing.

## Core Pages

- [Homepage](http://localhost:3000/): Product positioning and core value proposition.
- [Download](http://localhost:3000/download): Current installers and installation guidance.
- [Mission](http://localhost:3000/mission): Project mission, contributors, and sponsors.
- [Pricing](http://localhost:3000/pricing): Licensing and pricing details.
- [Sponsor](http://localhost:3000/sponsor): Sponsorship options and support details.
- [Roadmap](http://localhost:3000/roadmap): Planned work and project direction.
- [Brand](http://localhost:3000/brand): Public brand assets and usage details.
- [Linux waitlist form](http://localhost:3000/form): Linux availability notifications form.

## Installers And Release Metadata

- [Network installer](https://download.tiles.run/tiles-0.4.7-signed.pkg): Latest macOS installer, version 0.4.7, size 67.75 MB, sha256 2c17999f25d34d78a9e4e21100d8ebdb8daf15fe375a2a10ec8c6f27c2195d9b.
- [Offline installer](https://download.tiles.run/tiles-0.4.7-full-signed.pkg): Bundled model gpt-oss-20b-MXFP4-Q4, size 10.31 GB, sha256 e2fa2d5339d356c023fb1c13fba8a6cf099fedad07f684b7b090d59292c91032.
- [Offline installer checksum file](https://download.tiles.run/checksums/tiles-0.4.7-full-signed.pkg.sha256): SHA256 checksum file for offline installer validation.
- [Changelog](http://localhost:3000/changelog): Release notes and links to previous versions.

## Documentation (Book)

- [Book index](http://localhost:3000/book): Technical docs for Tiles and Tilekit.
- [Tiles Book](http://localhost:3000/book): Technical documentation covering the models, infrastructure, and cryptography behind Tiles, the consumer product, and Tilekit, the developer-facing SDK written in Rust.
- [Community](http://localhost:3000/book/community): See how you can make Tiles better.
- [Licenses](http://localhost:3000/book/licenses): Understand Backer and Commercial licenses, payment terms, and purchasing options.
- [Manual](http://localhost:3000/book/manual): Command-line interface reference for Tiles, with usage examples and links to additional guides
- [Memory](http://localhost:3000/book/memory): Our work on memory model research for on-device context and memory management.
- [MIR Extension](http://localhost:3000/book/mir): Machine Intelligence Resource naming schema extension for AIGC/ML work
- [Models](http://localhost:3000/book/models): Our approach to model selection and optimization
- [Overview](http://localhost:3000/book/overview): Tiles, our consumer product, and Tilekit, the SDK for developers.
- [Resources](http://localhost:3000/book/resources): A living index of resources that inform and inspire our work.
- [Security](http://localhost:3000/book/security): Security and privacy engineering in Tiles.
- [Tilekit](http://localhost:3000/book/tilekit): Tilekit CLI usage for Modelfiles, plus the complete reference for the Modelfile parser, grammar, instructions, parameters, and API usage

## Blog

- [Blog index](http://localhost:3000/blog): Blog landing page with all published posts.
- [Ship it up](http://localhost:3000/blog/ship-it-up): How we package and ship Tiles
- [Move Along, Python](http://localhost:3000/blog/move-along-python): Deterministic, portable Python runtimes for Tiles using layered venvstacks.

## Legal And Policy

- [Privacy](http://localhost:3000/privacy): Privacy statement for website and product.
- [Terms](http://localhost:3000/terms): Terms for using Tiles services and website.
- [Sub-processors](http://localhost:3000/sub-processors): Third-party processors used by the project.
- [Subprocessors alias](http://localhost:3000/subprocessors): Alias route for subprocessors page.

## Community And Feeds

- [Discord community](https://go.tiles.run/discord): Community support and discussion channel.
- [RSS feed](http://localhost:3000/api/rss): Syndicated feed for blog content.
- [GitHub organization](https://github.com/tilesprivacy): Source code and issue tracking.

## Optional

- [Brand assets archive](http://localhost:3000/tiles-brand-assets.zip): Public downloadable branding assets.
- [Status page](https://status.tiles.run): Service and infrastructure status.
